untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of calendar event (futures expiration day) and GARCH(1,1) variance equation coefficients with statistical significance testing (p<0.01). These are NOT available in the allowed indicators list. The signal also requires pre-computed GARCH ExpD coefficients for specific stocks (ACC, BHEL, BPCL, Dr. Reddy's, GRASIM, INFOSYS, ITC). Backtest engine does not support: (1) calendar-event triggering, (2) GARCH model estimation, (3) statistical significance thresholds. Approximated with india_vix spike as volatility proxy, but this does not capture the expiration-day specific effect.
translation_notes: GARCH volatility expansion on expiration day cannot be directly modeled with available indicators. Used india_vix as volatility proxy: entry when VIX > 20 (high volatility), exit when VIX < 15 (reversal). Hold_days_max set to 1 to match end-of-expiration-day exit. Direction set to BOTH because signal applies to both long and short volatility strategies. Stock filtering (specific NSE stocks) should be applied at backtest runtime. This is a simplified approximation—ideal implementation requires GARCH coefficient extraction or explicit expiration-day calendars.
